hypothesis: Enhancing steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions by introducing bulky aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will increase selectivity margin by differentiating SSTR2 from off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs show strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient selectivity. The focus should shift to disrupting off-target interactions at conserved positions while maintaining the pharmacophore and cysteine constraints.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved pocket contacts while preserving pharmacophore regions.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore region (positions 7-10). For example, introducing charged residues at positions 1 and 2 can repel off-target interactions.
